title: Can neuroscience help to understand narcissism? A systematic review of an emerging field.

assets.abstract: Narcissism is a Janusian personality construct, associated with both grandiose self-assuredness and dominance, as well as vulnerable insecurity and reactivity. Central questions of intra- and interpersonal functioning in narcissism are still a matter of debate. Neuroscience could help to understand the paradoxical patterns of experience and behavior beyond the limitations of self-reports. We provide a systematic review of 34 neuroscience studies on grandiose, vulnerable, pathological narcissism,

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Wind and pride — ego, narcissism, social dominance, self-esteem, status, social hierarchy in neuroscience, self-enhancement, hubris.
